strategy_description: Trend-following momentum strategy using 20 EMA / 50 EMA for direction and Fair Value Gaps (FVG) for precision entry. FVG is the WHERE. The trend is the WHY. Never trade an FVG against the higher-timeframe trend.

timeframes.trend_identification: Daily or 4H (one timeframe ABOVE entry TF)
timeframes.minimum_fvg_timeframe: 15m (never use FVGs on 1m or 5m — too noisy)

trend_identification.method: Four-Step Trend Checklist — ALL four must agree before trading
steps: 1. Price structure: Is price making Higher Highs + Higher Lows (uptrend) or Lower Highs + Lower Lows (downtrend)?
2. 20 EMA slope: Is it pointing upward (bullish) or downward (bearish)?
3. 50 EMA slope: Is it confirming the 20 EMA direction?
4. Price position: Is price trading ABOVE both EMAs (bullish) or BELOW both (bearish)?
trend_identification.rule: If MAs and price structure DISAGREE — do NOT trade. Market is transitioning or ranging.

bullish: 20 EMA is ABOVE 50 EMA (Golden Cross confirmed)
Price is trading above both EMAs
Price structure shows consecutive Higher Highs and Higher Lows (HH + HL)
BTC daily trend is bullish (use as leading indicator for ETH/alts)
bearish: 20 EMA is BELOW 50 EMA (Death Cross confirmed)
Price is trading below both EMAs
Price structure shows consecutive Lower Highs and Lower Lows (LH + LL)
neutral_no_trade: MAs are flat, intertwined, or crossing back and forth
Price is oscillating between horizontal support and resistance (range)
20 EMA and price structure are disagreeing in direction
Market is transitioning between trend phases

entry_rules.step_1_trend: Confirm higher-timeframe trend using Four-Step Checklist. LONG bias only in uptrend. SHORT bias only in downtrend.

step_2_pullback.description: Wait for a structured, controlled pullback AGAINST the trend direction toward the 20 EMA or 50 EMA zone
step_2_pullback.ideal_depth_percent: 38% to 62% of the most recent impulse wave

pullback_depth_guide.under_38pct: Very strong trend — high-probability entry zone
pullback_depth_guide.38_to_62pct: Normal healthy pullback — standard entry zone, highest frequency
pullback_depth_guide.62_to_786pct: Trend weakening — trade only with extra confirmation
pullback_depth_guide.over_786pct: Possible trend reversal — DO NOT TRADE, reassess trend

step_2_pullback.note: Do NOT anticipate the end of the pullback. Wait for FVG confirmation in step 3.

step_3_fvg_identification.description: Look for a valid FVG within the pullback zone on the entry timeframe

bullish_fvg.definition: 3-candle structure: Large bullish impulse candle (C2) between C1 and C3
bullish_fvg.zone: High of Candle 1 → Low of Candle 3 (Low of C3 must be ABOVE High of C1)
bullish_fvg.requirement: Must appear DURING or just AFTER the pullback — not during the original impulse
bullish_fvg.entry: Enter when price pulls back INTO this zone

bearish_fvg.definition: 3-candle structure: Large bearish impulse candle (C2) between C1 and C3
bearish_fvg.zone: Low of Candle 1 → High of Candle 3 (High of C3 must be BELOW Low of C1)
bearish_fvg.entry: Enter when price retraces UP into this zone

step_3_fvg_identification.confirmation_candle: Wait for a bullish close OFF the lower boundary of a bullish FVG before entering (reduces false entries)

step_4_stop_loss.long: Place BELOW the low of the entire FVG zone
step_4_stop_loss.short: Place ABOVE the high of the entire FVG zone
step_4_stop_loss.buffer: Add 0.5% to 1.0% beyond the FVG boundary to avoid noise stop-outs
step_4_stop_loss.formula: Stop Loss = FVG Low − (FVG Low × 0.005) for longs
step_4_stop_loss.rule: NEVER place stop loss INSIDE the FVG zone. NEVER move stop loss away from profit once set.

step_5_take_profit.preferred_target: Most recent swing high (for longs) or swing low (for shorts) before the pullback began
step_5_take_profit.scaling: Optional: take 50% profit at 2:1 target, trail stop to breakeven for the remainder
step_5_take_profit.breakeven_rule: Trail stop to breakeven once price has moved 1:1 in your favor

step_6_execute.entry_method: Limit order at FVG zone boundary, or market order on confirmation candle close
after_entry: Immediately set stop loss on exchange — before anything else
Set alerts at stop loss, take profit, and midpoint
Step away — do not watch every candle

risk_management.position_size_formula: Position Size = (Account Balance × Risk%) / Stop Loss Distance%
risk_management.stop_loss_distance_formula: |Entry Price − Stop Loss Price| / Entry Price × 100
risk_management.rr_minimum: 2:1 — never take a trade below this

asset_risk_guide.ETHUSD: 1% risk — similar to BTC, slightly higher volatility
asset_risk_guide.BNBUSD: 0.5% risk — follows BTC, exchange risk factor
asset_risk_guide.XRPUSD: 0.5% risk — regulatory news risk, sudden moves
asset_risk_guide.ADAUSD: 0.25%–0.5% risk — lower liquidity, wide stops needed

risk_management.news_rule: No trading within 2 hours of major news events (Fed announcements, CPI data, earnings)
risk_management.losing_streak_rule: After 2 consecutive losses in a day, stop trading for the rest of that session

fvg_rules_and_warnings.rule_1: FVG is an ENTRY TOOL, not a signal. The trend is WHY you trade. The FVG is WHERE you enter.
fvg_rules_and_warnings.rule_2: NEVER trade a bullish FVG in a downtrend. NEVER trade a bearish FVG in an uptrend.
fvg_rules_and_warnings.rule_3: Wait for a confirmation candle close within the FVG zone — do not enter the moment price touches it.
fvg_rules_and_warnings.rule_4: Only use FRESH FVGs formed during the current pullback. Ignore FVGs older than a few days.
fvg_rules_and_warnings.rule_5: Stop loss must go BELOW the entire FVG zone — never inside it.
fvg_rules_and_warnings.rule_6: Minimum timeframe for valid FVG: 15 minutes. Prefer 1H and 4H for swing trades.
fvg_rules_and_warnings.rule_7: Larger FVG zone = more aggressive institutional order = higher fill probability.
fvg_rules_and_warnings.rule_8: An FVG in the middle of a strong trend opposite to your bias is NOT a trade signal.

part_A_market_context: Check BTC trend on Daily chart — above or below 20/50 EMA?
Note any major news events today (Fed, CPI, earnings)
Review overnight price action for significant moves
Identify market phase: trending, range-bound, or transitioning
part_B_asset_scan: Open watchlist: BTC, ETH, SOL, Gold, tracked alts
For each asset: run Four-Step Trend Checklist on Daily and 4H
Mark assets in clear trends as 'Active Watch'
For Active Watch assets: identify and draw current FVG zones on 4H
Mark ranging or transitioning assets as 'No Trade' for the session
part_C_trade_plan: Write down which assets to trade and in which direction
Set price alerts at FVG zone levels
Pre-calculate position sizes using risk formulas
Confirm max trade count for the session (1–3 maximum)
post_trade_review: Record in trading journal: entry, exit, P&L, context
Did I follow my rules exactly? If not, where did I deviate?
Was the setup A+, B, or C quality?
What can I learn from this trade regardless of outcome?

before_every_trade: Higher TF trend confirmed via MA alignment AND price structure
Pullback depth is 38%–62% of most recent impulse wave
Valid 3-candle FVG structure visible on entry timeframe
FVG direction matches trend — bullish FVG in uptrend only
Stop loss placed BELOW entire FVG zone with buffer
Position size calculated — max 1% account risk
No major news event in next 2 hours

notes: Primary strategy is Momentum + FVG Hybrid (Strategy 2 from manual). Use BTC daily trend as the leading indicator for ETH and altcoin direction. ETH moves 1.2x–1.5x BTC's percentage moves. SOL requires wider stops and smaller size due to 5%–15% daily moves. Gold forms the cleanest FVGs due to institutional involvement. Avoid overtrading — 5 quality setups at 2:1 R:R beats 25 mediocre ones every time. Execute the process. The profits follow.
